deep_dive.title: EvoPolicyGym: Evaluating Autonomous Policy Evolution in Interactive Environments
deep_dive.abstract: Autonomous agents are increasingly expected to improve executable policies through feedback, yet existing evaluations often collapse this process into a final score or confound it with open-ended software-engineering progress. We introduce Autonomous Policy Evolution, a controlled evaluation setting in which a harness-model agent repeatedly edits an executable policy system under a fixed interaction budget. We instantiate this setting in EvoPolicyGym, a benchmark built from compact interactive RL environments that evaluates how agents iteratively improve explored policies. On the EvoPolicyGym suite, GPT-5.5 achieves the strongest aggregate rank score and top-two performance on all 16 environments. Beyond leaderboard results, EvoPolicyGym also provides trajectory-level diagnostics that distinguish how agents allocate budget, convert feedback into parametric tuning. These analyses show that strong autonomous policy evolution depends not only on isolated task wins, but on discovering task-appropriate mechanisms and refining policies under bounded feedback.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates autonomous policy evolution, i.e., agents improving policies post-deployment, directly addressing post-training evaluation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估自主策略演化，即智能体部署后改进策略，直接针对后训练评估
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估自主策略演化，即智能体部署后改进策略，直接针对后训练评估
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估自主策略演化，即智能体部署后改进策略，直接针对后训练评估
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Autonomous Policy Evolution benchmark EvoPolicyGym to evaluate how agents iteratively improve policies through feedback, essential for post-training evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有评估将策略改进过程简化为单一分数的问题，提出自主策略演化评估框架EvoPolicyGym，在交互环境中让智能体反复编辑策略系统，定量衡量策略改进能力，基准测试显示GPT-5.5表现最佳。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有评估将策略改进过程简化为单一分数的问题，提出自主策略演化评估框架EvoPolicyGym，在交互环境中让智能体反复编辑策略系统，定量衡量策略改进能力，基准测试显示GPT-5.5表现最佳。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: techniques for post-training of autonomous agents

deep_dive.title: The CRISTAL Method: Neurosymbolic analysis from AI-synthesized world models
deep_dive.abstract: This project introduces the CRISTAL Method (Coherent Reliable Intentional Synthesis of Truthful Analysis Logic), a neurosymbolic framework for automating complex analysis workflows, with fundamental investment analysis as a primary use case. This domain poses major challenges: high structural uncertainty, noisy and subjective data, tight attention budgets, and the need for justified, reproducible decisions. Human analysts often struggle in this domain due to cognitive biases and limitations, suggesting significant value in automation. But while LLM-based agents have been proposed as analytical aids, their limitations -- poor numerical reasoning, unawareness of uncertainty, and lack of reproducibility -- hinder their effectiveness in this context. CRISTAL addresses these gaps through a principled blend of statistical model synthesis, continuous learning, and active learning. Starting from a natural-language prior knowledge curriculum, CRISTAL builds a dynamic, interpretable probabilistic program that enables full Bayesian inference, including uncertainty quantification and budget-aware data acquisition. CRISTAL continually refines its world model during analysis, leveraging LLMs for code synthesis and learning. We validate CRISTAL on a novel benchmark of synthetic equities with rich financial and textual data. On a company classification task, CRISTAL achieves Bayes-optimal accuracy with just 5 examples and a 5-second budget, outperforming state-of-the-art LLMs that plateau around 40\% accuracy even with order-of-magnitude more input data and compute.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: neurosymbolic agent automates fundamental investment analysis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 神经符号智能体自动化基本面投资分析
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 神经符号智能体自动化基本面投资分析
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 神经符号智能体自动化基本面投资分析
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CRISTAL, a neurosymbolic framework for automating investment analysis, addressing LLM agent limitations like poor numerical reasoning and lack of reproducibility, serving as a task-oriented financial agent.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM代理在投资分析中数值推理差、不确定性缺失等问题，提出神经符号框架CRISTAL，融合合成世界模型进行可靠分析。该框架作为任务导向的金融智能体，旨在实现结构化、可复现的投资决策，为金融领域智能体后训练提供了新的设计思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对LLM代理在投资分析中数值推理差、不确定性缺失等问题，提出神经符号框架CRISTAL，融合合成世界模型进行可靠分析。该框架作为任务导向的金融智能体，旨在实现结构化、可复现的投资决策，为金融领域智能体后训练提供了新的设计思路。

quick_skim.title: When Summaries Distort Decisions: Information Fidelity in LLM-Compressed Financial Analysis
quick_skim.abstract: Financial decision-makers face more information than they can directly inspect, making context compression necessary. Yet when large language models (LLMs) compress financial source material, they can alter the investment judgment supported by the original source. We frame this problem as information fidelity: compression loses fidelity when it changes the decision induced by the source. In agentic systems, such losses may recur across intermediate steps and amplify throughout the decision process. Across financial filings and earnings-call transcripts, we find that LLM-based compression can produce fluent and factually plausible compressed contexts that nevertheless alter downstream decisions. We analyze two diagnostic patterns associated with fidelity loss: decontextualization, where salient evidence is retained but separated from the caveats and contextual qualifiers needed for correct interpretation, and model dependency, where different compressors expose different views of the same source. We then propose Agentic Context Compression, which generates multiple candidate compressions and audits their disagreements against the original source. Our results suggest that financial compression should be evaluated not only by efficiency or factuality, but also by its ability to preserve decision-relevant context.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: investigates information fidelity of LLM-compressed financial analysis in agentic systems, relevant to financial agent decision-making
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究智能体系统中LLM压缩金融分析的信息保真度，与金融智能体决策相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究智能体系统中LLM压缩金融分析的信息保真度，与金融智能体决策相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究智能体系统中LLM压缩金融分析的信息保真度，与金融智能体决策相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that LLM-based compression of financial source material can distort downstream investment decisions in agentic systems, introducing the information fidelity problem.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 发现大语言模型压缩金融材料在智能体系统中可能改变投资决策，提出信息保真度概念，通过财务文件和电话会议分析证实压缩可能产生流畅但误导的上下文，影响决策过程。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 发现大语言模型压缩金融材料在智能体系统中可能改变投资决策，提出信息保真度概念，通过财务文件和电话会议分析证实压缩可能产生流畅但误导的上下文，影响决策过程。
